_comment: Fixture: network endpoints exercised by `cargo fetch` / `cargo build`
against an empty cache on Rust 1.70+ (sparse-index default).
Source of truth (documentation):
- cargo 1.70 release notes (sparse protocol default),
- https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/registry-index.html#web-api,
- https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/environment-variables.html
for the default `CARGO_REGISTRIES_CRATES_IO_PROTOCOL=sparse` setting.
Source of truth (live verification — M10-S8 todo #36, 2026-04-25):
Booted a sandbox VM with a hand-rolled policy permitting Ubuntu apt
mirrors + the three documented cargo hosts + the Fastly Anycast CIDR
inside the VM, then ran `cargo new` + `cargo add serde@1` + `cargo
fetch -v` against an empty CARGO_HOME. Captured mitmproxy events on
the gateway data plane and filtered to the cargo-phase window via an
RFC3339 timestamp marker. Observed cargo-phase hosts:
- index.crates.io  (sparse index lookups)
- static.crates.io (crate tarball downloads, ~7 crates pulled)
Did NOT observe `crates.io` (apex) — sparse mode bypasses the web-API
redirector and goes straight to the CDN. The apex is retained in the
preset because it remains required for `cargo publish`, `cargo
search`, `cargo yank`, and any tooling that hits the JSON web API.
Findings artifact (one-off): /tmp/m10s8-todo36-cargo-trace.json.
This file freezes the set of hosts the `cargo` built-in preset must allow.
The drift-detection unit test in `src/presets/builtin.rs` (see
`cargo_preset_matches_frozen_trace`) fails if the preset's host list
diverges from this fixture, forcing a conscious update with a
corresponding spec/docs review (D-9 + "Known gaps" bullet).
If a future cargo release introduces new telemetry / metrics hosts
(e.g. a sparse-index health-check endpoint or a publish-time
verification host), regenerate by running the live-trace methodology
above against the new toolchain and update both this fixture AND the
preset's host list in src/presets/builtin.rs::expand_cargo.

hosts.purpose: Sparse-protocol registry index. Default since Rust 1.70.
hosts.notes: Replaces the older git-based registry on crates.io-index.git. Cargo hits this first to resolve versions. Confirmed live (M10-S8 todo #36, 2026-04-25): observed during `cargo fetch` cargo-phase window.

hosts.purpose: Registry web API: search, yank, publish, owner management, and the `/api/v1/crates/<name>/<version>/download` redirector.
hosts.notes: Sparse-mode `cargo fetch` does NOT contact this host (verified live 2026-04-25 — sparse fetch goes index.crates.io → static.crates.io directly). Retained in preset because it remains required for `cargo publish`, `cargo search`, `cargo yank`, and any non-fetch web-API tooling.

hosts.purpose: CDN host that serves crate tarballs and (historically) RSS feeds.
hosts.notes: Confirmed live (M10-S8 todo #36, 2026-04-25): every crate tarball download (serde, serde_core, serde_derive, syn, quote, proc-macro2, unicode-ident) hit this host. Fronted by Fastly Anycast (151.101.0.0/16, 146.75.0.0/17) — see CARGO_FASTLY_CIDR_POOL in `expand_cargo` for IP-pinning rationale (todo #40).
